title: Faithful: PAEEarthquake param-response diverges from headless FCP (worst ddb 18.1 dB)
goal: FAITHFUL-REIMPL (ROADMAP Rule 13): the TS engine impl of PAEEarthquake must match REAL headless FCP across its ENTIRE parameter space, not just values the 65 fixed transitions use. The DELTA oracle (fct/faithful) set each continuous param of PAEEarthquake to range extremes across hosts ['Movements__Earthquake'] and compared how the oracle vs the engine RESPOND: ddb=PSNR(oracle(theta)-oracle(theta0), engine(theta)-engine(theta0)). worst ddb=18.10 < pass 40 means the engine responds WRONGLY to a real parameter change (a faithful engine scores ~99 dB; the identity/noise floor is 99). Fix the PAEEarthquake implementation so its parameter response matches FCP. NO scene-signature dispatch, NO env flags (Rule 12/13): behavior = f(filter params) only. Re-verify: `python3 -m fct.faithful.driver sweep PAEEarthquake` must reach >= pass_db worst-ddb across all hosts. Worst diverging (host/param/theta -> ddb): [{"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "randomseed", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.35, "oracle_signal": 13.496, "ddb": 18.1}, {"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "randomseed", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 23.304, "ddb": 20.782}, {"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.0, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 19.67, "ddb": 22.255}, {"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "layers", "theta": 2.0, "t": 0.35, "oracle_signal": 9.097, "ddb": 24.977}, {"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "layers", "theta": 2.0, "t": 0.65, "oracle_signal": 13.777, "ddb": 25.348}, {"host": "Movements__Earthquake", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.0, "t": 0.35, "oracle_signal": 13.057, "ddb": 25.814}]
